title: Urceolaria involucrata
intro: Urceolaria involucrata, also known as the "Indian snail's shell flower", is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Urceolaria involucrata include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Urceolaria involucrata is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. The Gesneriaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family is part of the larger order Lamiales, which includes a wide variety of flowering plants. Lamiales is a significant order within the angiosperm clade, containing approximately 24,000 species. Urceolaria involucrata is a unique and relatively rare species within its classification.
